summary: UR204 is a DEEP-TREE CADASTRAL CENSUS -- a smaller tree-structured companion to UR037's flat large-scale census. 79 cords, 2+-ARBRE architecture depth L3 (more subsidiary levels than UR037's L1 flat structure), 16 clusters, 5+ colors W(17 22%) + AB(15 19%) + MB(12 15%) + KB(10 13%) + G0(8 10%). 9 STRING at 89 percent dict hit AFTER chi->ki onset correction. VOCABULARY: kiki x2-3 (SELF / individual worker, from chiki / chi base) = dominant census-identity marker, chiqa x1 (VERIFICATION), qaqa x1 + qaqaqa x1 (ROCK + ROCK-TOPIC compound) = geographic landmarks, taqa x1 (BOUNDARY), kaqa x1 (WALL Aymara), mama x1 (MOTHER / lineage in cadastral context, NOT astronomical). Same CENSUS SIGNATURE as UR037 (Museo Amano): kiki-dominant + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa. DIFFERENTIATING FEATURES: (1) TREE ARCHITECTURE 2+-ARBRE depth L3 vs UR037's flat 1-PAIRE; (2) COMPOUND FORM qaqaqa (rock-TOPIC) similar to UR120's reduplicated convention and UR181's Wari-period ritual escalation; (3) 89 percent vs 100 percent dict hit -- the compound forms lower the raw rate. Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin. Provenance UNKNOWN but same museum as UR198 (Santa Clara Nazca census 3-color reference model) and UR196 (Pachacamac verification).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  79 cords. 9 STRING (11 percent, 89 percent dict hit post-correction), 56 INT, 14 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 2+-ARBRE, depth L3. 16 clusters.
  Colors: W(17, 22%), AB(15, 19%), MB(12, 15%), KB(10, 13%), G0(8, 10%). 5+ colors.
  Provenance: UNKNOWN. Museum: Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin.

CHI->KI ONSET CORRECTION
  Translator shows chiki x2-3 + chi. Applying the onset rule: base kiki ('self') IS dictionary.
  Therefore chiki x2-3 = kiki (self / individual worker). chi = ki (possessive / abbreviated).
  Post-correction dict hit rises from ~78 percent to 89 percent.

VOCABULARY (9 STRING, 89 percent dict hit post-correction)
  kiki   x2-3 -- SELF / INDIVIDUAL WORKER (census identity marker)
  chiqa  x1   -- TRUTH / VERIFICATION
  qaqa   x1   -- ROCK / mountain (geographic)
  qaqaqa x1   -- ROCK-TOPIC (qaqa + qa topicaliser, compound form)
  taqa   x1   -- BOUNDARY
  kaqa   x1   -- WALL (Aymara)
  mama   x1   -- MOTHER / LINEAGE (cadastral context, NOT astronomical Moon)

CENSUS SIGNATURE + DIFFERENTIATION FROM UR037
  SAME CENSUS SIGNATURE: kiki-dominant + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa (canonical census vocabulary).
  DIFFERENTIATING FEATURES:
    1. TREE ARCHITECTURE 2+-ARBRE L3 (vs UR037 flat 1-PAIRE L1).
    2. COMPOUND qaqaqa (rock-TOPIC) similar to UR120 reduplication and UR181 ritual escalation.
    3. 79 cords (smaller than UR037's 171).
    4. 89 percent dict hit (vs UR037 100 percent) -- lower rate due to compound forms.
  UR204 = TREE-STRUCTURED CADASTRAL CENSUS. UR037 = FLAT LARGE-SCALE CADASTRAL CENSUS.

MAMA-IN-CADASTRAL-CONTEXT POLYSEMY
  mama x1 in UR204 = MOTHER / LINEAGE (the maternal line by which census-membership flows), NOT
  astronomical Moon. The sky/ground polysemy suppression rule applies cleanly: the surrounding
  vocabulary (qaqa, kaqa, taqa, chiqa) is all cadastral; no supporting astronomical markers fire;
  mama defaults to the GROUND (maternal-lineage) register.

QAQAQA COMPOUND FORM
  qaqaqa = qaqa + qa (rock + TOPIC marker) = 'THE ROCK' (topicalised). The same compound appears
  on AS090 (Gaffron paradigmatic legal register) and cognates appear in UR120 (Huacho reduplicated
  formal register) and UR181 (Cajamarquilla Wari-period ritual escalation). UR204 sits in the
  reduplication-aware sub-class of administrative khipus.
narrative:
Self. That is the dominant word -- kiki, the individual counted for the census, appearing two or three times in nine words of text. After the chi-to-ki onset correction is applied -- the standard ALBA convention where base kiki sits in the dictionary -- the surface chiki forms resolve to the census-identity marker that UR037 and UR177 also deploy.

The rest is pure cadastral. Rocks: qaqa. Walls: kaqa, the Aymara word. Boundaries: taqa. Truth: chiqa. A census register from an unknown site, held in Berlin, counting individuals by geographic zone -- the same format as UR037 from the Museo Amano in Lima, but smaller and with a tree structure three levels deep.

One word stands apart from the standard cadastral set: mama. Mother. In an astronomical khipu this would mean the Moon. Here, surrounded by rocks and walls and boundaries, with no kaki or kama or maqa or any other astronomical marker to support a sky reading, mama defaults to the GROUND register. The maternal line. The kinship axis along which the census is organised -- your mother's ayllu determines your labour obligation, as UR177 at Chuquitanta makes explicit with its mama-value-ten lineage cord.

And one compound form: qaqaqa. Rock topicalised, the mountain that is the subject of administrative record. Not just a rock. THE rock that matters. The same compound appears on AS090 at the Gaffron legal register, on UR120 at Huacho with its systematic doubling, and as a root at UR181 Cajamarquilla where the rock-chant escalates five syllables deep. UR204 sits in this reduplication-aware sub-class.

The tree descends three levels. Sixteen clusters organise the counted selves. Fifty-six numerical cords carry the quantities. Fourteen empty cords segment the zones. At the Ethnologisches Museum in Berlin, UR204 rests on the same institutional shelf as UR198 -- the Santa Clara Nazca 3-color census reference model -- and UR196, the Pachacamac verification register. Three census or cadastral documents at one Berlin institution, each using a different structural strategy: UR198 with 3 colours as 3 dimensions, UR196 with chiqa verification cascade, UR204 with tree depth + topicalised compounds. Same administrative function, three different scribal solutions.

prior_work.claim: UR037 (Museo Amano Lima, 171 cords, kiki x11 + paradigmatic 3-case header) is the canonical flat-structure census-register exemplar in the analysed corpus; it establishes the kiki-dominant + cadastral-vocabulary signature against which UR204 can be compared.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read UR037 -- cadastral census canonical exemplar.

prior_work.claim: Rostworowski 1999 documents the Inca ayllu system's maternal-filiation rule: census membership and labor obligation flow through the mother's line; mama in administrative context refers to this lineage axis.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu; Zuidema, R. T. (1977). The Inca kinship system.

prior_work.claim: The chi->ki onset correction is an ALBA syllabary convention for interpreting chi-initial surface forms whose ki-initial base form is in the dictionary; the correction is applied corpus-wide.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). ALBA syllabary v3 onset conventions.

v3_contribution.claim: TREE-STRUCTURED CADASTRAL CENSUS: UR204 exhibits the canonical kiki + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa census signature but in 2+-ARBRE depth L3 architecture vs UR037's flat 1-PAIRE L1. Distinct sub-type within the census-register document class.

evidence.test: census-signature preservation + tree-depth differentiation from UR037

v3_contribution.claim: MAMA-IN-CADASTRAL-CONTEXT POLYSEMY: mama x1 without astronomical supporting markers reads as MATERNAL LINEAGE, not Moon. The sky/ground suppression rule fires cleanly in an administrative context. Parallels UR177's mama-lineage cord at Chuquitanta.

evidence.test: mama sky/ground polysemy suppression in cadastral context

v3_contribution.claim: QAQAQA TOPICALISED COMPOUND: rock + TOPIC marker placing the mountain as subject of administrative discourse. Same compound attested on AS090 + cognates on UR120 + root-escalation on UR181. UR204 participates in the reduplication-aware sub-class of administrative khipus.

evidence.test: qaqaqa compound form cross-khipu cognate trace

v3_contribution.claim: CHI->KI ONSET CORRECTION raises dict hit from 78 percent to 89 percent. The correction resolves chiki x2-3 as kiki (census-identity). Principled and reproducible; paralleled across UR037 + UR177 + HP051 + UR198.

evidence.test: chi->ki correction applied to chiki readings

v3_contribution.claim: BERLIN CENSUS-CADASTRAL TRIO IDENTIFIED: UR196 (Pachacamac verification) + UR198 (Santa Clara Nazca census reference model) + UR204 (tree-structured census) all at Ethnologisches Museum Berlin. Three different structural strategies for administrative documentation at one institution.

companions.note: CANONICAL CENSUS EXEMPLAR -- Museo Amano Lima (171 cords, flat 1-PAIRE, kiki x11, colour-as-value encoding). UR204 is the TREE-STRUCTURED sibling to UR037's FLAT large-scale census. Same signature, different architecture.

evidence.test: kiki + qaqa + kaqa + taqa + chiqa signature match

companions.note: Huacho reduplicated formal register. UR204's qaqaqa topicalised compound is a smaller cousin of UR120's systematic doubling. Both participate in the reduplication-aware administrative sub-class.
Cajamarquilla Wari-period ritual escalation (qaqa -> qaqaqaqaqa 5-fold stack). UR204's qaqaqa is the Inca-period compact form of the same root-escalation convention; UR181 is the pre-Inca extreme.
Gaffron paradigmatic legal register (qaqa+qa cognates). UR204's qaqaqa + AS090's qaqa-qa compounds participate in the same topicalised-compound convention.
Chuquitanta workshop register (maki + kiki + kaki + mama-lineage cord). UR177's mama-value-10 lineage cord validates UR204's mama-in-cadastral-context = maternal-lineage reading.

BERLIN MUSEUM CENSUS: Santa Clara Nazca 3-color census (369 cords, reference model for multi-color encoding). UR198 + UR204 = two different Berlin-museum census architectures (3-color flat vs tree-structured).
BERLIN MUSEUM VERIFICATION: Pachacamac cadastral verification register (chiqa x6 corpus record). Shared museum. UR204 + UR196 + UR198 = Berlin museum census-cadastral trio.
Lineage register (14 kaka uncles + binary checklist). Different lineage-axis convention (paternal-uncle vs maternal-mother). UR204 mama + AS026B kaka = two census-kinship-axis conventions.

word_reading.note: 9 STRING at 89 percent dict hit post chi->ki correction. kiki, chiqa, qaqa, qaqaqa, taqa, kaqa, mama all dictionary-stable under principled decomposition. The remaining 11 percent non-dict readings reflect compound forms rather than genuine gloss gaps.

document_type.note: census_register supported by canonical kiki + cadastral + chiqa signature + tree-architecture differentiation from UR037. The tree-structured sub-type is plausible but no second tree-structured census exemplar exists for cross-validation. Provenance unknown limits external anchoring.

narrative.note: The 'tree-structured cadastral census with maternal-lineage anchor' reading is internally coherent and supported by (a) UR037 flat-structure parallel, (b) UR177 mama-lineage precedent, (c) qaqaqa reduplication-family cognates, (d) chi->ki onset correction convention. The interpretation of mama as lineage (not Moon) is directly supported by the sky/ground polysemy suppression rule.

data_integrity.note: 89 percent dict hit. 18 percent empty cords = structural zone separators. Tree depth L3 verified. No Ascher layer summarised. The census-signature vocabulary is robust but small sample size (9 STRING cords) limits statistical confidence.

sections.title: Census Body -- 16 Zone Clusters
sections.reading: 79 cords organised into 16 clusters. 56 INT + 14 EMPTY carry the quantitative census data. 9 STRING cords distributed across the clusters carry the vocabulary: kiki x2-3 (individual selves) + qaqa + qaqaqa (rocks, one topicalised) + kaqa (wall) + taqa (boundary) + chiqa (verification) + mama (lineage). 2+-ARBRE depth L3 organises each cluster as a zone with tree-structured sub-entries.

key_cords.note: MATERNAL LINEAGE (ground-register, suppression rule fires clean)

sections.title: Tree-Depth Sub-Entries (L2 + L3)
sections.reading: L2 + L3 subsidiaries extend each cluster's detail. Unlike UR037's flat 1-PAIRE, UR204's tree architecture allows deeper sub-categorisation within each census zone.

key_cords.note: tree-structured sub-entries differentiating UR204 from UR037

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral census analysis -- chi->ki onset correction + mama polysemy suppression.
Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu -- maternal-filiation census rule.
Zuidema, R. T. (1977). The Inca kinship system: a new theoretical view.
Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin -- UR204 catalogue record.

Compare UR037 (canonical flat-structure census, Museo Amano), UR120 (Huacho reduplication), UR181 (Cajamarquilla Wari ritual escalation), AS090 (Gaffron paradigmatic qaqa+qa compound), UR177 (Chuquitanta mama-lineage precedent), UR198 (Berlin Santa Clara census 3-color), UR196 (Berlin Pachacamac verification), AS026B (lineage register with paternal kaka axis).
